expectations.text: Correctly identifies B as higher CTR with clear reasoning
expectations.evidence: The response opens unambiguously: 'Verdict: B gets dramatically higher CTR' and provides a detailed multi-section analysis explaining why across 4 distinct principles.

expectations.text: Explains the role of 'World's Longest' as a scale/superlative trigger
expectations.evidence: Section 'Scale as a Trigger' explicitly states: "'World's' is a scale word — it implies the entire globe has been considered and this surpasses everything. Scale language reliably drives clicks because it signals something genuinely unusual is being shown."

expectations.text: Thumbnail concept for B emphasizes scale (person dwarfed by pasta, measurement visible)
expectations.evidence: The thumbnail concept for B states: 'An aerial or wide-angle shot showing a single strand of pasta stretching impossibly far... A person at one end looks tiny next to the pasta, creating an immediate sense of scale.' This directly matches the expectation.

expectations.text: Notes that thumbnail A has no obvious visual hook
expectations.evidence: The section on Option A states: 'There is no obvious visual hook. Homemade pasta looks like... pasta... is an unremarkable image... even a great photo here won't create curiosity.' Also in the summary table: 'Visual hook: None.'

expectations.text: References the principle that extremity outperforms neutral framing
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Extremity Outperforms Neutral Language — Always' explicitly states: 'The more extreme or superlative a title sounds, the higher the curiosity pull.' This is a direct invocation of the skill's core extremity rule.

claims.claim: Option A would have a CTR of 2-4% and Option B 8-15%+
claims.evidence: These are reasonable industry estimates for YouTube CTR ranges, but specific numbers cannot be verified without actual YouTube analytics data. They serve as illustrative benchmarks.

claims.claim: The thumbnail and title are a 'unit' that must work together
claims.evidence: The 'Thumbnail/Title Alignment' section of the response explicitly discusses how the thumbnail and title form a contract and must point at the same thing — consistent with skill guidance.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions. The evals are solid and the with_skill response genuinely demonstrates deep application of skill principles. All expectations are met with substantive, non-superficial evidence.
